title: Public HTTPS Demo Deployment

executionPolicy.contract: All Sprint 7 implementation, deployment, verification, status updates, and handoffs must use this JSON file as the canonical task contract.
updateRules: Only one task should normally be in_progress at a time unless work is explicitly parallel and independent.
Before starting implementation, update the target task status to in_progress and set the first actionable step to in_progress.
When a step is completed, set its status to done and add concrete evidence such as command output summary, URL, screenshot path, or config path.
When all required steps and acceptance checks for a task pass, set the task status to done.
If a task cannot proceed, set status to blocked and record blocker, owner, and nextAction.
Do not mark the sprint done until every required task is done or explicitly skipped with a reason.
securityRules: Do not write the DuckDNS token into this JSON, Markdown, Caddyfile, shell history, commit message, or logs.
Do not use self-signed certificates as public demo acceptance evidence.
Do not use curl -k as acceptance evidence for trusted HTTPS.
Do not expose backend port 3001 or Postgres port 5432 to the public internet.
Do not configure Caddy with tls internal for the public demo domain.

objective: Deliver a reproducible public demo deployment for LingoBridge at https://lingobridge.duckdns.org with a publicly trusted HTTPS certificate and working microphone/camera browser permissions.

recommendedRoute.primary: Caddy Automatic HTTPS with HTTP-01 challenge
recommendedRoute.secondary: Caddy custom build with DuckDNS DNS-01 challenge only if HTTP-01 is impossible
fallbacks: Temporary non-mainland cloud host with trusted HTTPS
Trusted HTTPS tunnel such as Cloudflare Tunnel or ngrok for short demo rescue
Formal domain plus ICP filing for long-term mainland production

keyResults.acceptance: DuckDNS token is rotated, old token no longer works, and no real token appears in repository files or logs.
lingobridge.duckdns.org resolves to 101.34.72.227 from public DNS.
Tencent Cloud allows public 80/443; backend 3001 and Postgres 5432 are not publicly exposed.
Browser opens https://lingobridge.duckdns.org without certificate warning; issuer is a public CA such as Let's Encrypt or ZeroSSL.
Health endpoint, login, course page, Live Class creation, and upload resources work over HTTPS with no mixed content.
window.isSecureContext is true and getUserMedia succeeds on desktop Chrome and at least one mobile browser.
Rollback command, troubleshooting notes, public URL, cert issuer, and evidence paths are recorded.

tasks.title: DuckDNS token safety rotation
tasks.objective: Remove the exposed DuckDNS token from the usable security boundary before any further deployment work.

steps.title: Rotate DuckDNS token in the DuckDNS console
evidence: Token rotated by user in DuckDNS console. Old token invalidated.

steps.title: Update the new token only in the server secret store if DNS-01 or IP update automation needs it
steps.command: Use server .env, Docker secret, or protected environment variable; do not commit the value.
steps.blocker: No SSH access to Tencent server yet. Token will be stored on server during S7-T07 deployment.
evidence: Token received from user. Will be stored securely on server during S7-T07 (deploy step).

steps.title: Search repository files for DuckDNS token leakage
evidence: No real DuckDNS token found in repository files. Only sprint spec references to hostname and provider name.

steps.title: Confirm no real token is present in the new sprint Markdown or JSON files
evidence: No real DuckDNS token in sprint files. Only spec/academic references to "token" (security rules, acceptance criteria).

acceptance: Old exposed token is no longer usable.
No real DuckDNS token is stored in repository files.
If a token is required, it is stored only in a protected runtime secret location.
tasks.nextAction: Proceed to S7-T02: Discover Tencent Cloud resources.

tasks.title: Discover Tencent Cloud resources with tccli
tasks.objective: Identify the exact Tencent Cloud instance, region, public IP, and security groups before changing networking or deployment configuration.

steps.title: Log in to Tencent Cloud CLI
evidence: tccli already authenticated with API key and region ap-guangzhou

steps.title: Verify TCCLI works
evidence: DescribeRegions returned 18 regions successfully

steps.title: Record active TCCLI config
steps.command: tccli configure list && tccli configure get region
evidence: region=ap-guangzhou, SecretId/SecretKey present, output=json

steps.title: Query likely CVM regions
evidence: No CVM instances found in any queried region (ap-guangzhou, ap-shanghai, ap-beijing, ap-hongkong, ap-nanjing, ap-chengdu, ap-chongqing)

steps.title: Query likely Lighthouse regions if CVM does not identify the instance
evidence: Found 1 Lighthouse instance in ap-shanghai (ap-shanghai-5)

steps.title: Record instance metadata
evidence: InstanceId: lhins-jn9scrkw
InstanceName: Ubuntu-uZ5A
PrivateIp: 10.0.0.17
Zone: ap-shanghai-5
Type: Lighthouse (CVM-alternative)
OS: Ubuntu Server 22.04 LTS 64bit
CPU: 4 vCPU, Memory: 4GB, Disk: 40GB SSD
State: RUNNING
No security group IDs (Lighthouse uses firewall rules instead)

acceptance: Region is known.
Instance ID is known.
Public IP matches or explains 101.34.72.227.
Security group IDs are recorded.
tasks.nextAction: Authenticate TCCLI and locate the instance.

tasks.title: Verify public DNS
tasks.objective: Confirm the demo hostname resolves to the target public IP before attempting public certificate issuance.

steps.title: Query A record with dig
Query A record with nslookup
evidence: lingobridge.duckdns.org -> 101.34.72.227

steps.title: Compare DNS result with Tencent instance public IP
evidence: DNS resolves to 101.34.72.227, matches Lighthouse instance PublicIpAddresses

steps.title: If DNS is stale, update DuckDNS IP and wait for propagation
steps.command: Manual DuckDNS console update or protected update script.
evidence: DNS already correct (101.34.72.227). No update needed.

steps.title: Probe existing HTTP health endpoint
evidence: Via domain: 302 redirect to dnspod block page (China ISP intercept, expected pre-HTTPS)
Via IP:80: nginx/1.31.1 301 Moved Permanently (server is running nginx)
Via IP:3001: empty response (backend not publicly available)
HTTPS:443: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL (no certificate yet)

acceptance: DNS resolves to the intended server public IP.
Any mismatch has a documented cause and remediation.
Current HTTP behavior is documented before HTTPS migration.

tasks.title: Harden Tencent Cloud and host firewall ports
tasks.objective: Allow ACME and public HTTPS while keeping internal services private.

steps.title: Inspect Tencent security group ingress rules
evidence: Lighthouse firewall rules inspected: TCP 22/80/443/8443/18789 open to 0.0.0.0/0; ICMP open

steps.title: Allow public TCP 80 and 443
steps.command: Add ingress rules for 80/tcp and 443/tcp from 0.0.0.0/0 and ::/0 if IPv6 is used.
evidence: 80/tcp (HTTP) and 443/tcp (HTTPS) already allowed from 0.0.0.0/0. Ready for ACME HTTP-01 challenge.

steps.title: Confirm SSH remains controlled
steps.command: Prefer limiting 22/tcp to trusted source IPs.
evidence: SSH (22/tcp) is currently open to 0.0.0.0/0. Documented as non-ideal; consider restricting after deployment is stable.

steps.title: Confirm backend and database are not publicly exposed
steps.command: Check that 3001/tcp and 5432/tcp are not allowed from public internet.
evidence: No firewall rule allows 3001/tcp (backend) or 5432/tcp (Postgres) from public internet. Confirmed secure.

steps.title: Inspect server listening ports
steps.blocker: Requires SSH access to server (lhins-jn9scrkw). Will be done during S7-T07 deploy.

acceptance: 80/tcp and 443/tcp are reachable from public internet.
3001/tcp and 5432/tcp are not publicly reachable.
No unknown service occupies 80/443 before Caddy deployment.
tasks.nextAction: Inspect and update security group rules.

tasks.title: Add Caddy HTTPS ingress configuration
tasks.objective: Replace or front the current Nginx HTTP ingress with Caddy Automatic HTTPS for the public demo domain.

steps.title: Create docker/Caddyfile for lingobridge.duckdns.org
evidence: docker/Caddyfile created with lingobridge.duckdns.org, SPA, /api/* and /uploads/* reverse proxy to backend:3001, HSTS headers

steps.title: Configure Caddy to serve dist as SPA static frontend
evidence: Caddyfile uses try_files {path} /index.html + file_server for SPA routing

steps.title: Configure /api/* reverse proxy to backend:3001
evidence: Caddyfile handle_path /api/* reverse_proxy backend:3001

steps.title: Configure /uploads/* reverse proxy to backend:3001
evidence: Caddyfile handle_path /uploads/* reverse_proxy backend:3001

steps.title: Update Docker Compose to run Caddy on 80 and 443 with persistent data volumes
evidence: docker-compose.yml: nginx replaced with caddy (80:80, 443:443)
docker-compose.https.yml: created as HTTPS deployment override variant
Persistent volumes: caddy-data, caddy-config

steps.title: Disable or remove old Nginx ingress to prevent port conflict
evidence: nginx service removed from docker-compose.yml (replaced by caddy)
nginx config files retained in docker/nginx/ for rollback reference

steps.title: Verify no self-signed public TLS config is present
evidence: No self-signed TLS config found in docker/ directory. Caddy uses default Automatic HTTPS.

acceptance: Caddy config contains lingobridge.duckdns.org as a public site address.
Caddy maps 80 and 443.
Caddy /data and /config are persistent volumes.
Nginx does not conflict with Caddy.
No tls internal or self-signed public certificate config is used.

tasks.title: Build frontend with HTTPS API base
tasks.objective: Ensure the production frontend never calls HTTP, localhost, 127.0.0.1, or public IP APIs.

steps.title: Build frontend with explicit HTTPS API base
evidence: Build succeeded (2758 modules, 4m23s). API base: https://lingobridge.duckdns.org/api/v1

steps.title: Search source and dist for unsafe production endpoints
evidence: No unsafe endpoints found in dist. Source uses VITE_API_BASE_URL env var with HTTPS default for production build.

steps.title: Confirm API calls use apiClient or same-origin HTTPS
evidence: API calls go through apiClient.ts which uses VITE_API_BASE_URL env var (https://lingobridge.duckdns.org/api/v1 for production build)

steps.title: Run baseline validation before server deploy
steps.command: npm run lint && npm run build
evidence: lint (tsc --noEmit) passed with no errors
Build succeeded with VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://lingobridge.duckdns.org/api/v1

acceptance: Build succeeds.
Production dist has no unsafe API origin.
Lint and backend tests pass or failures are documented with blocker status.
tasks.nextAction: Run local validation and fix unsafe origins if found.

tasks.title: Deploy HTTPS stack on Tencent server
tasks.objective: Apply code and Docker ingress changes on the Tencent Cloud server in a reproducible way.

steps.title: SSH into the target server and inspect current repository state
Pull the latest code
Install dependencies
Build frontend for HTTPS domain
Start Docker stack
Inspect running services
Inspect Caddy logs for ACME status

acceptance: Docker stack is up.
Caddy container is running.
Backend and Postgres are healthy or functional.
Caddy logs show certificate automation progress with no fatal ACME errors.
tasks.nextAction: SSH into server, inspect repo state, pull code, build, and deploy Docker stack.

tasks.title: Verify public trusted HTTPS certificate
tasks.objective: Prove the public domain uses a publicly trusted certificate and redirects HTTP to HTTPS.

steps.title: Verify HTTP redirects to HTTPS
Verify HTTPS succeeds without insecure mode
Inspect certificate chain
Record issuer, SAN, and expiry
steps.expected: Issuer is public CA; SAN contains lingobridge.duckdns.org.

steps.title: Open in a normal browser and confirm no certificate warning
steps.command: Manual browser verification or Browser plugin screenshot.

acceptance: HTTP returns 301 or 308 to HTTPS.
HTTPS curl works without -k.
Certificate issuer is a public CA, not Caddy Local Authority.
Browser shows no certificate warning.
tasks.nextAction: Run certificate verification after Caddy starts.

tasks.title: Run HTTPS application smoke
tasks.objective: Confirm the application works over the trusted HTTPS domain, including the recently fixed Live Class flow.

steps.title: Verify health endpoint over HTTPS
Verify homepage loads over HTTPS
steps.command: Manual browser or Browser plugin verification.

steps.title: Log in as teacher
Open course page and add a Live Class
steps.expected: No Course not found regression in Postgres mode.

steps.title: Verify upload or existing uploaded resource URL loads over HTTPS
steps.expected: No mixed content and no HTTP resource URL.

steps.title: Log in as student and verify course/live/homework visibility
Check browser console for mixed content

acceptance: Health endpoint is green over HTTPS.
Teacher can add Live Class on public HTTPS domain.
Student sees expected course/live/homework entries.
No mixed content blocks core workflows.
tasks.nextAction: Run app smoke after certificate verification.

tasks.title: Run microphone and camera secure-context smoke
tasks.objective: Prove the public HTTPS deployment satisfies browser security requirements for recording/camera demo features.

steps.title: Confirm secure context in browser
Confirm MediaDevices API exists
Request microphone permission on desktop Chrome
Request camera and microphone permission if camera demo is required
steps.command: await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true, audio: true })

steps.title: Run app-level recording workflow
steps.command: Manual app smoke on the public HTTPS domain.

steps.title: Run mobile browser microphone smoke
steps.command: Manual mobile smoke, preferably on mobile network.

steps.title: Document device, browser, permission result, and any workaround

acceptance: window.isSecureContext is true.
navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia exists.
Desktop Chrome microphone capture succeeds.
Mobile microphone capture succeeds on at least one device.
Camera capture succeeds if needed for the demo scope.
tasks.nextAction: Run browser media tests after app smoke.

tasks.title: Document troubleshooting and rollback evidence
tasks.objective: Make the deployment recoverable and handoff-safe for later agents or humans.

steps.title: Record rollback to previous HTTP Nginx stack
steps.command: cd /opt/lingobridge/docker && docker compose stop caddy && docker compose up -d nginx backend postgres

steps.title: Record rollback to known-good git commit
steps.command: git checkout <known-good-commit> && npm install && VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://lingobridge.duckdns.org/api/v1 npm run build && cd docker && docker compose up -d --build

steps.title: Record Caddy data volume preservation rule
steps.expected: Do not delete caddy-data or caddy-config unless deliberately resetting ACME state.

steps.title: Record the top failure modes encountered during deployment

acceptance: Rollback commands are documented and tested or clearly marked untested.
Troubleshooting notes include actual observed failures and fixes.
Caddy certificate persistence requirement is documented.
tasks.nextAction: Fill in after deployment and smoke checks produce real evidence.

tasks.title: Finalize Sprint 7 delivery record
tasks.objective: Close the sprint only when the public demo milestone is genuinely achieved or the remaining blockers are explicit.

steps.title: Update global evidence fields
Set all passed keyResults to done
List non-blocking risks
List any blocked or skipped tasks with owner and next action
Mark sprint status done only if all required tasks and KRs are done

acceptance: Public URL is recorded.
Trusted certificate issuer is recorded.
HTTPS app smoke evidence is recorded.
Media device smoke evidence is recorded.
Rollback and troubleshooting notes are recorded.
Sprint status accurately reflects reality.
tasks.nextAction: Finalize after all P0 deployment and smoke tasks complete.

doneDefinition: https://lingobridge.duckdns.org/ opens in a normal browser without certificate warning.
Teacher can add a Live Class on the public HTTPS domain.
Student can access the relevant course/live/homework entries.
window.isSecureContext is true on the public domain.
getUserMedia microphone smoke succeeds on desktop and mobile.
DuckDNS token has been rotated and no real token is committed.
Rollback commands and evidence are recorded.

statusLog.change: Created canonical Sprint 7 JSON execution contract from sprint-07-public-https-demo-deploy.md.
